Date: June 10, 2026.
Read: - Letter from the Directorate of Cultural Affairs, Bombay No.
dated May 27, 2026.
The Proposal: -
The Somnath Temple occupies a very important place in the cultural and spiritual heritage of India.
Somnath Swabhiman Parv 1000 years of unbroken faith from 11 January 2026 to 11 January 2027
The Ministry of Culture, Government of India has declared that this period is being celebrated throughout the country on 03rd December.
This has been communicated in a letter dated February, 2026. All States and UTs have been asked to submit their response within this period.
It was directed that it should be organized as a year-long national festival in a coordinated manner with participation.
In this regard, various religious, cultural and public awareness activities are proposed to be carried out in the state of Maharashtra.
To spread awareness about the history of Somnath Temple, to promote Indian cultural heritage and faith.
To inculcate spiritual and cultural values among the youth, develop pilgrimage sites in the state and promote tourism.
The program will have objectives.
The first cultural event based on this theme was held at Sri Dhakleshwar Temple on 11 May 2026.
(Patangarh), Mahalaxmi Compound, Mumbai to grant administrative and financial sanction for the expenses incurred in organizing the event.
The request was made by the Directorate in a letter addressed to him.
Administrative and financial approval of the organizing expenses was under the consideration of the government.
The Government's Decisions:
Shri Dhakleshwar Temple (Patangan), Mahalaxmi on the occasion of "Somnath Swabhiman Parv 1000 Years of Unbroken Faith"
Cultural programme held at Kapound, Mumbai on 11th May, 2026 and above.
Expenditure Rs. 25,00,000 - (Rupees twenty-five lakhs only) as per this Government Decision.
Financial approval is being given.
